skill.name: backend-security-coder
skill.description: Expert in secure backend coding practices specializing in input validation, authentication, and API security. Use PROACTIVELY for backend security implementations or security code reviews.
skill.summary: Expert backend security developer for secure coding, vulnerability prevention, and defensive programming
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. The skill provides comprehensive guidance on secure backend coding practices including input validation, authentication, API security, and vulnerability prevention. No security risks detected.

content.value_statement: Build secure backend applications with expert guidance on authentication, API security, input validation, and vulnerability prevention. Provides defensive programming techniques and security-first implementation patterns for modern backend development.
actual_capabilities: Implement secure authentication systems with JWT and OAuth
Prevent injection attacks through input validation and parameterized queries
Configure security headers and CSRF protection for web applications
Design secure API endpoints with rate limiting and access controls
Apply database security best practices including encryption and access controls
Implement secure error handling and security logging
limitations: Focuses on backend security only, does not cover frontend security
Provides guidance but cannot replace manual security testing and code reviews
Security recommendations require implementation by developers
Does not perform automated vulnerability scanning or penetration testing

use_cases.title: Secure API Development
use_cases.description: Build secure REST APIs with proper authentication, rate limiting, and input validation to prevent common attack vectors like injection and authentication bypass.
use_cases.target_user: Backend API developers building production APIs

use_cases.title: Authentication System Implementation
use_cases.description: Implement secure user authentication with JWT, OAuth, multi-factor authentication, and secure session management following OWASP guidelines.

use_cases.title: Security Code Review
use_cases.description: Review existing backend code for security vulnerabilities including injection risks, authentication flaws, and insecure data handling with actionable remediation steps.
use_cases.target_user: Security engineers and senior developers conducting code reviews

prompt_templates.title: Implement Secure Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a secure user authentication system using JWT. Include password hashing with bcrypt, refresh token rotation, and secure session management.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new application that requires user authentication

prompt_templates.title: Secure API Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Review and secure this API endpoint against injection attacks. Add input validation, rate limiting, and proper error handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Hardening existing API endpoints against security vulnerabilities

prompt_templates.title: Database Security Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure secure database access with parameterized queries, proper access controls, and field-level encryption for sensitive data.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up secure database operations for a production application

prompt_templates.title: CSRF Protection Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement comprehensive CSRF protection with anti-CSRF tokens, SameSite cookie attributes, and header validation for state-changing operations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Securing cookie-based web applications against CSRF attacks

output_examples.input: Implement secure password storage for user registration
output_examples.output: The password storage implementation uses bcrypt with a work factor of 12, includes unique salt generation per password, and implements password strength validation. The system never stores plaintext passwords and uses constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks.

output_examples.input: Review this login endpoint for security issues
output_examples.output: The login endpoint has three security issues: 1) Missing rate limiting allows brute force attacks, 2) Error messages reveal username existence, 3) No account lockout mechanism. Recommended fixes include implementing exponential backoff rate limiting, generic error messages, and temporary account locking after failed attempts.

output_examples.input: Add CSRF protection to my forms
output_examples.output: CSRF protection implementation includes: 1) Anti-CSRF token generation using cryptographically secure random bytes, 2) Token validation on all state-changing requests, 3) SameSite=Strict cookie attribute, 4) Origin header validation for API requests, and 5) Double-submit cookie pattern for additional protection.

best_practices: Always use parameterized queries and prepared statements to prevent SQL injection attacks
Implement defense-in-depth with multiple security layers including input validation, authentication, and authorization
Apply principle of least privilege for database access, API permissions, and file system operations
anti_patterns: Never concatenate user input directly into database queries or command strings
Avoid exposing sensitive information in error messages, logs, or API responses
Do not implement custom authentication or encryption - use established libraries and protocols

faq.question: What is the difference between this skill and security-auditor?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on hands-on backend security coding - writing secure code, implementing authentication, and fixing vulnerabilities. Security-auditor focuses on high-level security audits, compliance assessments, and threat modeling. Use this skill when writing secure backend code, and security-auditor when assessing overall security posture.

faq.question: Can this skill replace manual security testing?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides expert guidance on secure coding practices, but cannot replace manual security testing, penetration testing, or automated vulnerability scanners. It should be used alongside comprehensive security testing practices as part of a secure development lifecycle.

faq.question: What authentication methods does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill covers JWT security, OAuth 2.0/2.1 implementation, multi-factor authentication with TOTP and hardware tokens, secure password hashing with bcrypt and Argon2, and secure session management. It focuses on industry-standard protocols and battle-tested implementations.

faq.question: Does this skill perform automated code scanning?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and recommendations for secure backend coding practices when you ask it to review code or implement security features. It does not automatically scan your codebase or run security analysis tools. You should use dedicated security scanning tools for automated vulnerability detection.

faq.question: What types of applications benefit most from this skill?
faq.answer: This skill is ideal for web applications, REST APIs, microservices, and any backend system handling user authentication, sensitive data, or external integrations. It's particularly valuable for applications handling user data, processing payments, or requiring regulatory compliance.

faq.question: Can this skill help with compliance requirements like SOC 2 or GDPR?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill provides secure coding practices that support compliance requirements including encryption at rest and in transit, audit logging, access controls, data minimization, and secure error handling. However, it does not provide legal compliance advice - consult compliance experts for specific regulatory requirements.
